display.purpose: Section, panel, and dialog headings only — no oversized hero type.

body.purpose: Default reading size across every view; 13.5px, dense by design.

label.purpose: Uppercase table headers and structural metadata exclusively — never a decorative eyebrow.

mono.purpose: IDs, hashes, ports, log tails, inspect JSON.

shadows.name: card-elevation
shadows.purpose: Primary data surfaces — tables, main content cards. Not used on secondary chrome.

shadows.name: selection-rail
shadows.purpose: Selected table row / active sidebar item indicator — inset rail, never a border-side stripe.

shadows.name: focus-ring
shadows.purpose: Keyboard focus on buttons, inputs, interactive rows.

motion.name: eng-dot-ring
motion.purpose: Soft pulsing halo on the live-engine status dot only.

motion.name: button-press
motion.purpose: Tactile press feedback on buttons — a nudge, not a bounce or scale.

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: The single primary action per view — filled Refined Lime.
components.css: .ds-btn-primary { background: #7fb53a; color: #11210a; height: 32px; padding: 0 10px; border-radius: 0.45rem; font-size: 13px; font-weight: 500; border: none; transition: background-color .15s, transform .1s; } .ds-btn-primary:hover { background: color-mix(in srgb, #7fb53a 80%, transparent); } .ds-btn-primary:focus-visible { outline: none; box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px color-mix(in srgb, #7fb53a 50%, transparent); } .ds-btn-primary:active { transform: translateY(1px); }

components.name: Success Button
components.description: Soft-filled lifecycle action — used for container start. Color encodes consequence, not brand.
components.css: .ds-btn-success { background: color-mix(in srgb, #179a5f 10%, transparent); color: #179a5f; height: 32px; padding: 0 10px; border-radius: 0.45rem; font-size: 13px; font-weight: 500; border: none; transition: background-color .15s; } .ds-btn-success:hover { background: color-mix(in srgb, #179a5f 20%, transparent); }

components.name: Destructive Button
components.description: Soft-filled lifecycle action — used for container stop / remove.
components.css: .ds-btn-destructive { background: color-mix(in srgb, #cf3f50 10%, transparent); color: #cf3f50; height: 32px; padding: 0 10px; border-radius: 0.45rem; font-size: 13px; font-weight: 500; border: none; transition: background-color .15s; } .ds-btn-destructive:hover { background: color-mix(in srgb, #cf3f50 20%, transparent); }

components.name: Default Badge
components.css: .ds-badge { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; height: 20px; padding: 2px 8px; border-radius: 999px; background: #7fb53a; color: #11210a; font-size: 12px; font-weight: 500; white-space: nowrap; }

components.name: Status Dot
components.description: Quiet status indicator — always paired with a word + muted sub-line in markup. Never a glowing pill.
components.css: .ds-status-dot { display: inline-block; width: 7px; height: 7px; border-radius: 999px; background: #179a5f; flex-shrink: 0; }

components.name: Data Table Card
components.description: Primary data surface — container/image/volume/network tables. The one place the layered elevation shadow appears.
components.css: .ds-card { background: #15171d; border: 1px solid rgba(255,255,255,.065); border-radius: 0.65rem; overflow: hidden; box-shadow: 0 1px 2px rgba(0,0,0,.45), 0 10px 28px -12px rgba(0,0,0,.6), inset 0 1px 0 rgba(255,255,255,.04); } .ds-card-row { display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 8px; padding: 13px; color: #f3f4f6; font-size: 13.5px; }

components.name: Sidebar Nav Item (active)
components.description: Active sidebar entry — inset lime rail, never a border-side stripe.
components.css: .ds-nav-item { display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 8px; width: 100%; height: 32px; padding: 0 10px; border-radius: 0.45rem; background: transparent; color: #aab1bb; font-size: 13px; border: none; text-align: left; } .ds-nav-item--active { background: #15181d; color: #f3f4f6; box-shadow: inset 2px 0 0 #a6e35b; }

narrative.overview: dockwin's interface is the workshop of someone who builds tools for their own use first: dense, accurate data surfaces (container tables, live stats, log tails, inspect JSON) housed in a shell that's warm and rounded rather than terminal-cold, but never decorated past the point of usefulness. Every visual choice answers to the product's thesis — the wrapper around Docker Engine should be as free and unencumbered as the engine itself — which translates directly into design restraint: no chrome for chrome's sake, no upsell surfaces, no glow for glow's sake. The system explicitly rejects Docker Desktop's heavy, nag-laden chrome; it rejects the generic AI-tool aesthetic of ambient lime-on-dark glow and glowing filled status pill-bands; and it rejects the cookie-cutter SaaS admin template — identical card grids, gradient text, uppercase eyebrow labels. dockwin reads as crafted by a single attentive developer, not generated from a dashboard kit. Dark is the hero surface (the default), with light fully first-class — both driven by the same token set via the .dark class, so neither is a second-class afterthought.
keyCharacteristics: Dense, table-and-stat-driven data views, not marketing-style whitespace
One accent color (lime), deployed in exactly four roles, never as ambient decoration
Quiet status communication: a small dot, one word, a muted sub-line — never a glowing badge
Native-desktop feel over webby SaaS feel: thin themed scrollbars, system typography, no user-select sprawl
Dark-first, light fully supported, both built from the same semantic token set

rules.name: The One Voice Rule
rules.body: Refined Lime appears in exactly four places per screen: primary action, active-nav rail, selected-row rail, one focused chart. Anywhere else it shows up is a bug, not a feature.

rules.name: The Lifecycle Override
rules.body: Start/stop container actions are the one deliberate exception to brand-accent restraint: start = emerald (success button variant), stop = destructive red — borrowed from status color, not lime — because the color needs to encode the action's consequence, not the brand.

rules.name: The Structural Uppercase Rule
rules.body: Uppercase, letter-spaced labels are reserved for table headers and structural metadata. They are never used as a decorative eyebrow above section headings.

rules.name: The One Lift Rule
rules.body: The layered card shadow is reserved for primary data surfaces (tables, main panels). Secondary chrome stays flush against its parent surface, distinguished by a hairline border, not a second shadow language.

dos: Do keep Refined Lime to its four roles: primary action, active-nav rail, selected-row rail, one focused chart.
Do use the soft-filled success/destructive button pattern for container start/stop — color encodes consequence, not brand.
Do communicate status with a dot + word + muted sub-line.
Do reserve uppercase, letter-spaced label type for structural metadata, never as decorative section eyebrows.
Do keep the layered card shadow exclusive to primary data surfaces; everything else stays flush with a hairline border.
Do support both themes as first-class — dark is the hero/default, light is fully realized.
donts: Don't let lime become ambient decoration — no lime backgrounds, no lime glows beyond the single top-edge backdrop gradient.
Don't build glowing filled status pill-bands.
Don't reach for Docker Desktop's pattern of heavy chrome, nags, or upsell surfaces.
Don't ship identical card grids, gradient text, or tiny uppercase tracked eyebrow labels above section headings.
Don't use a border-left/border-right colored stripe as a status or selection indicator — use the inset shadow rail instead.
